title: ShuffleNetV2 trunk with FiLM-conditioned single field and top-K decode replaces the two-expert MobileNet blend
hypothesis: 16 straight reverted rounds (36-52) all churned the location-decision mechanism (softmax reparameterizations, attention consensus, spatial MoE, hard VQ coding, continuous Gaussian mixture) while leaving champion's ImageNet-MobileNetV3-Small trunk untouched, and every one of them landed well below champion (743.07 m) -- several catastrophically (1000000000.0 m x6, 2140-2751 m x3). The ONE round that instead changed the trunk and kept the field-decode family (exp 42, domain-native contrastive from-scratch trunk) came closest of all 17 post-champion rounds at 846.42 m (+14%), the tightest gap in the entire losing streak. That single data point, plus the uniform failure of every decode-family alternative, points at descriptor information content -- not the decision mechanism -- as the real wall: champion's two hard-gated experts (a layout-aware head and a GAP-only 'dark' head, blended by a sigmoid trained on raw brightness) are a coarse patch over a trunk whose SE-gated inverted-residual features were learned for natural photographs, not confusable low-resolution aerial texture, and whose lighting handling is a binary switch rather than a continuous read of illumination. With that backbone banned this round regardless, the experiment worth running is: does a structurally different pretrained feature family (grouped convolutions + channel shuffle, no depthwise-separable/SE blocks), combined with continuous rather than discrete lighting conditioning, close more of the gap than any of the 11 decode rewrites did -- while also fixing the one real mismatch the champion's own field never addressed: the frozen scorer's soft-argmax reads across all 1024 cells even though only a handful are ever genuinely competitive, letting a confident-looking distant lookalike quietly pull the answer toward it.
method: Four coupled changes, one coherent design: (1) model/model.py's trunk is replaced with an ImageNet-pretrained ShuffleNetV2 x0.5 (torchvision, BSD-3) truncated to stem+stage2+stage3 (stride 16, ~96ch @ 8x8 for a 128x128 input) -- a structurally different backbone family from MobileNetV3, loaded from a new local weights asset model/pretrained/shufflenetv2_x0_5_stage3.pt. (2) The two hard-gated field experts (bright head + dark head + sigmoid blend) are deleted; one field FC reads a single descriptor built from GAP + the existing 1x1 spatial-layout squeeze, continuously modulated via FiLM (scale/shift from a tiny MLP over the crop's own mean luminance) instead of a binary day/night gate. (3) The four C4 rotation views are fused at the feature level (element-wise max over the 4 views' FiLM-modulated descriptors) before the single field FC runs once per crop, replacing the old late fusion of two already-blended heatmaps. (4) Decode is restricted to a top-48-of-1024 masked soft-argmax after sharpening (was: full 1024-way soft-argmax), and confidence is recomputed directly from that shortlist's normalized entropy through a 2-parameter calibrated score, replacing the old 4-feature learned confidence MLP. Training (model/train.py) gets three matching changes: confusability-weighted sampling (exp 35) is computed per (cell, lighting bucket) using each bucket's own rendered texture instead of one global reference-image weighting; loss_fn gains one new term, a margin penalty when the true location's cell is not already inside the model's own top-48 shortlist, so the training signal explicitly targets what the new decode reads; and the LR schedule adds a 500-step linear warmup before cosine decay, with the pretrained-trunk LR ratio dropped from 10x to 20x below the head LR.
expected_outcome: If descriptor information content is really the dominant wall (not the decision mechanism), this should land meaningfully closer to or below champion's 743.07 m -- a materially better result than exp 42's 846.42 m, since exp 42 paired a stronger trunk-swap idea with the old two-expert blend and full-field decode, neither of which this design keeps unexamined. Landing in the 800-950 m range (comparable to or modestly better than exp 42) would still be informative: it would show trunk-family swaps are the main lever and the top-K decode / FiLM changes are neutral-to-helpful refinements, not the deciding factor. A sharp regression (>1200 m) with healthy training signals (stable NLL/CE descent, no NaNs, per-bucket keep rate >= 40% at calibration, top-48 shortlist actually containing the true cell on a majority of crops) would newly implicate ShuffleNetV2's grouped-conv features specifically as a poor fit for this task, distinct from the FiLM/top-K decode choices, and would argue for trying a different pretrained family or a from-scratch trunk next round while keeping this round's FiLM + top-K decode machinery.
eli5: Every one of the last 16 tries kept the same borrowed off-the-shelf 'photo recognition' brain and only changed how the model turns its guesses into a final answer -- and none of them won. The one time someone swapped the brain itself instead came closest to beating the champion. So this time we swap in a differently-built starter brain (ShuffleNet instead of MobileNet -- same idea of starting from a brain pretrained on ordinary photos, but organized in a structurally different way, like hiring someone with a different training background rather than re-coaching the same specialist). We also replace the old on/off 'is this a night photo?' light switch -- which forced two separate day-expert and night-expert opinions to be blended -- with a dial that continuously reads how dark the shot is. And when the model commits to an answer, it now only lets its 48 most-likely spots (out of 1024 possible) compete for the final vote, instead of letting one confident-looking but wrong far-away lookalike quietly drag the answer toward it.

stages.name: Camera frame
stages.detail: 128×128 px aerial crop, one of 6 simulated lighting conditions

stages.name: Rotation fan
stages.detail: the crop and its three exact 90° pixel rotations all pass through the shared trunk; instead of computing a separate heat map per rotation and averaging the four finished maps afterward, the four rotations' own feature descriptors are combined by taking the strongest (element-wise max) response across all four views before any location guess is made — a lookalike that only looks confusing from one specific rotation gets outvoted at the feature level, not softened by averaging four full guesses

stages.name: Feature extractor
stages.detail: ImageNet-pretrained ShuffleNetV2 x0.5 (torchvision, BSD-3), stem + first two stages (stride 16, ~96 channels on an 8×8 grid) — a grouped-convolution + channel-shuffle design, structurally unrelated to MobileNetV3's depthwise-separable + squeeze-excite blocks

stages.name: Lighting-conditioned descriptor
stages.detail: the trunk's GAP summary plus the existing 1x1-squeezed spatial layout code, continuously modulated (scaled and shifted) by a tiny network that reads only the crop's own average brightness — replaces the old hard day-expert/night-expert split with one descriptor that dials in how dark the shot is instead of switching between two

stages.name: Probability map
stages.detail: a single field layer turns the lighting-conditioned, view-fused descriptor into one probability map over the 1,024 map cells — no second expert, no gate blend, one field per crop

stages.name: Decode
stages.detail: the map's contrast is sharpened, then only its 48 strongest cells (of 1,024) are allowed to compete; the answer is the balance point among just that shortlist, so a confident-looking but wrong far-off cell outside the shortlist can no longer pull the answer toward it

stages.name: Confidence
stages.detail: read directly off how spread out that 48-cell shortlist is (a clean single hotspot vs. several rivals still in contention), turned into a calibrated sure/unsure score by two learned numbers — replaces the old 4-feature learned confidence network

stages.name: Training data
stages.detail: the confusability-weighted place sampling from round 35 now measures which places look alike separately for EACH of the 6 lighting conditions (using that condition's own rendered image), instead of one shared measurement taken only from the daytime reference image

stages.name: Training signal
stages.detail: the usual grading (reward probability near the true spot, grade the committed answer, grade the confidence guess) plus one new rule: if the true spot's cell isn't already inside the model's own top-48 shortlist, that's penalized directly — so training explicitly keeps the right answer competitive for the shortlist the decode step actually reads, instead of just rewarding it somewhere in the full 1,024-cell field

stages.name: Training schedule
stages.detail: learning rate ramps up gently for the first 500 steps before the usual cosine glide to zero, and the pretrained trunk is nudged 20x more gently than the rest of the network (was 10x) — the new trunk and lighting-conditioned descriptor need steadier early adaptation than the old two-expert blend did

implementation_brief:
FIXED CONTRACTS TO PRESERVE EXACTLY: train.py's CLI (--area --out-dir --data-dir --epochs --max-crops-per-bucket --seed) does not change. model/model.py's ONNX export contract does not change: build_model() takes no args, export_onnx(model, path) exports a 1x3x128x128 float32 [0,1]-range input named 'frame' to one output named 'uvc' of shape [1,3] = [[u, v, conf]] with u,v in normalized [0,1] map coords and conf in [0,1]. Keep the trunk submodule attribute named self.features on the model class (train.py's train_area does `trunk_params = list(model.features.parameters())` to build the two-LR-group optimizer -- if this attribute name changes, train.py breaks). Keep the `conf_shift` registered buffer with that exact name and keep pred[:,2] a sigmoid(z - conf_shift) value -- train.py's calibrate_conf_shift() only touches model.conf_shift and reads output[:,2]/logit of it; do not change that function.

model/model.py changes:
1. Delete the `from torchvision.models import mobilenet_v3_small` import and PRETRAINED_TRUNK_PATH's mnv3s_features8.pt reference. Add `from torchvision.models import shufflenet_v2_x0_5`. New constant: `PRETRAINED_TRUNK_PATH = Path(__file__).parent / 'pretrained' / 'shufflenetv2_x0_5_stage3.pt'`.
2. One-time asset prep (do this yourself before training, it is not runtime code): `m = shufflenet_v2_x0_5(weights=ShuffleNet_V2_X0_5_Weights.IMAGENET1K_V1); trunk = torch.nn.Sequential(m.conv1, m.maxpool, m.stage2, m.stage3); torch.save(trunk.state_dict(), 'model/pretrained/shufflenetv2_x0_5_stage3.pt')`. If this environment cannot reach the network to fetch torchvision's ImageNet weights, fall back to `shufflenet_v2_x0_5(weights=None)` for the same truncated Sequential (no local weights file, no state_dict load) and change init_strategy in your own head to from-scratch when you report back -- both are acceptable per the rules, only reusing the champion's actual weights blob or constructor is banned.
3. Replace `_build_pretrained_trunk()` to build `nn.Sequential(shufflenet_v2_x0_5(weights=None).conv1, ...maxpool, ...stage2, ...stage3)` and strict-load the new state dict (no key stripping needed since we save the Sequential's own state_dict directly, not a slice of the full model's prefixed keys).
4. New constant `TOPK = 48`.
5. In TinyLocNet.__init__: delete `self.dark_logits` and `self.gate`. Keep `self.layout_squeeze` (Conv2d, re-probe feat_ch/fmap_hw exactly as the existing code already does after building self.features, since ShuffleNetV2's channel count differs from MobileNetV3's). Add a FiLM MLP: `self.film = nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(1, 16), nn.ReLU(), nn.Linear(16, 2 * feat_ch))`. Replace `self.loc_logits` with a single `self.field = nn.Linear(feat_ch + layout_ch * fmap_hw * fmap_hw, grid_k * grid_k)`. Replace `self.conf_head` with two learnable scalars, e.g. `self.conf_a = nn.Parameter(torch.tensor(1.0)); self.conf_b = nn.Parameter(torch.tensor(0.0))`. Delete nothing else (norm_mean/norm_std buffers, cell_u/cell_v buffers, conf_shift buffer all stay).
6. Rewrite forward(): keep `lum = x.mean(dim=(1,2,3)).unsqueeze(1)` and `x4 = _c4_stack(x)` and `fmap = self.features((x4 - norm_mean)/norm_std)` unchanged. Compute `f4 = fmap.mean(dim=(2,3))` and `layout4 = self.layout_squeeze(fmap).flatten(1)` as before (still per-view, batch dim 4N). Compute FiLM params ONCE from lum (not lum4, since luminance is identical across the 4 rotations): `gamma, beta = self.film(lum).chunk(2, dim=1)`; repeat each 4x to match the 4N batch (`gamma4 = gamma.repeat(4,1)`, same for beta4), then `f4 = f4 * (1 + gamma4) + beta4` (FiLM applied to the GAP descriptor only, not layout4 -- mirrors exp 12's reasoning that the cheap/robust signal should carry the lighting adaptation, not the spatial code). Reshape f4 and layout4 to (4, N, -1) and fuse with `.max(dim=0).values` (not `.mean(dim=0)`) for both. Concatenate the fused f and fused layout, pass through `self.field(...)` to get `logits` [N, 1024] directly (no 4-way reshape+mean needed afterward, since fusion already happened before the FC). Delete the old `p1`/`u1`/`v1` unsharpened-decode computation entirely (no longer used by the new confidence mechanism). Decode: `p = torch.softmax(DECODE_BETA * logits, dim=1)`; `topk_val, topk_idx = torch.topk(p, TOPK, dim=1)`; `topk_norm = topk_val / topk_val.sum(dim=1, keepdim=True)`; `u = (topk_norm * self.cell_u[topk_idx]).sum(dim=1, keepdim=True)`; same for v with self.cell_v. Confidence: `ent = -(topk_norm.detach() * torch.log(topk_norm.detach() + 1e-9)).sum(dim=1, keepdim=True) / float(np.log(TOPK))`; `z = self.conf_a * (1.0 - ent) + self.conf_b`; `conf = torch.sigmoid(z - self.conf_shift)`. Return `torch.cat([u, v, conf], dim=1)` (and logits if return_logits, as before).
7. loss_fn(pred, logits, target_uv): keep the existing Gaussian-smoothed CE term and coord L2 term and BCE conf term completely as-is (they are generic over pred/logits shapes and need no edits). Add one new term right after the CE computation: `logp = torch.log_softmax(DECODE_BETA * logits, dim=1)`; `true_idx = g.argmax(dim=1)`; `logp_true = logp.gather(1, true_idx.unsqueeze(1)).squeeze(1)`; `kth_logp = torch.topk(logp, TOPK, dim=1).values[:, -1]`; `coverage_loss = torch.relu(kth_logp - logp_true).mean()`. Change the return to `return ce + coord_loss + 0.3 * conf_loss + 0.2 * coverage_loss`.

model/train.py changes:
1. compute_cell_weights(area, data_dir, meta): add a required `bucket` parameter; replace the hardcoded `area_dir(area, data_dir) / 'reference.tif'` read with `area_dir(area, data_dir) / 'relight' / f'{bucket}.png'` opened via PIL (`np.asarray(Image.open(...))`, already imported) instead of rasterio (no georeferencing needed for a plain PNG the way reference.tif's rasterio read was used -- just need the HxWx3 uint8 array, so drop the `.transpose(1,2,0)` rasterio-specific step and read straight as HxWx3). Everything else in the function (block-splitting, descriptor, nearest-lookalike distance with the Chebyshev neighbor exclusion) is untouched.
2. train_area(): replace the single `cell_w = compute_cell_weights(area, data_dir, meta)` / single `crop_probs` block with a per-bucket dict: `cell_w_by_bucket = {b: compute_cell_weights(area, data_dir, meta, b) for b in LIGHTING_BUCKETS}`, then for each bucket build `raw_w = cell_w_by_bucket[b][crop_cell]`, `weighted = raw_w / raw_w.sum()`, `crop_probs_by_bucket[b] = CONFUSABILITY_ALPHA * weighted + (1 - CONFUSABILITY_ALPHA) * uniform`, renormalized. Pass `crop_probs_by_bucket` (the dict) to sample_epoch instead of the single `crop_probs` array.
3. sample_epoch(): change the `crop_probs` parameter to `crop_probs_by_bucket` (dict keyed by bucket name); inside the `for bucket in LIGHTING_BUCKETS:` loop, use `p=crop_probs_by_bucket[bucket]` in the `rng.choice(...)` call instead of the single shared array. No other change to this function.
4. Optimizer construction in train_area(): change the trunk LR from `1e-4` to `5e-5` (20x below the head's `1e-3`, was 10x).
5. LR schedule: replace the single `torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(opt, T_max=total_steps, eta_min=0.0)` with a warmup-then-cosine schedule, e.g. `warmup = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.LinearLR(opt, start_factor=1e-3, total_iters=500); cosine = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(opt, T_max=max(total_steps - 500, 1), eta_min=0.0); sched = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.SequentialLR(opt, schedulers=[warmup, cosine], milestones=[500])`. Everything else in the epoch/step loop (sched.step() call site, optimizer.step(), etc.) is unchanged.

After implementation, run `.venv/bin/python -c "import model.train, model.model"` and sanity check that no identifier from {mobilenet_v3_small, mnv3s_features8.pt} remains anywhere under model/ (there should be zero occurrences outside the historical docstring log, which is fine since the check strips docstrings).
